title: Terraform Testing
learningObjectives: Write tests using the native terraform test framework (1.6+)
Use run blocks with assert conditions and command types
Mock providers for unit-level testing without real infrastructure

theory.content:
Terraform Test Framework

Terraform 1.6 introduced a native testing framework with .tftest.hcl files. Tests can verify infrastructure behavior, validate outputs, and check module correctness without relying on third-party tools like Terratest.

Test File Structure

# tests/main.tftest.hcl

# Optional: override variables for tests
variables {
  environment    = "test"
  instance_count = 1
}

# Plan-only test (no real infra created)
run "validate_plan" {
  command = plan

  assert {
    condition     = aws_instance.app.instance_type == "t3.micro"
    error_message = "Wrong instance type for test environment"
  }
}

# Apply test (creates real infra)
run "create_and_verify" {
  command = apply

  assert {
    condition     = output.instance_ip != ""
    error_message = "Instance IP should not be empty"
  }
}

Provider Mocking (Terraform 1.7+)

# tests/mock_test.tftest.hcl
mock_provider "aws" {
  mock_resource "aws_instance" {
    defaults = {
      id        = "i-mock12345"
      public_ip = "1.2.3.4"
    }
  }
  mock_data "aws_ami" {
    defaults = {
      id = "ami-mock123"
    }
  }
}

run "unit_test_with_mocks" {
  command = plan
  assert {
    condition     = aws_instance.app.ami == "ami-mock123"
    error_message = "AMI should match mocked data source"
  }
}

Running Tests

# Run all tests
terraform test

# Run specific test file
terraform test -filter=tests/main.tftest.hcl

# Verbose output
terraform test -verbose

Test Modules

# Reference a specific module in test
run "test_vpc_module" {
  module {
    source = "./modules/vpc"
  }
  variables {
    cidr_block  = "10.0.0.0/16"
    environment = "test"
  }
  assert {
    condition     = output.vpc_id != ""
    error_message = "VPC ID must be set"
  }
}
